steps.title: Identify the student's specific requirement
steps.content: The student's goal is to CONTRAST the purposes of the two maps. This means the answer must explicitly state what each map's purpose is and show how they differ.

steps.title: Match the student's need to the choices
steps.content: Choice A directly states both purposes and contrasts them: the opening map introduces readers to the fictional world they are about to enter, while the closing map allows them to reconstruct the story they have just read. Choices B and D omit the purposes (focusing on publication details instead), while Choice C mentions purposes without explaining what they are. That matches choice A — A is correct.
